name: Biology
description: An introduction to living systems — from cell biology and genetics to evolution, ecology, and the diversity of life on Earth.

modules.title: The Chemistry of Life
modules.objectives: Describe how atoms, chemical bonds, water properties, and the four major macromolecules support living systems, then connect each molecule to its monomer and biological function.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 2: The Chemistry of Life
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Elements and Atoms
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #1 — That's Why Carbon Is A Tramp
modules.assignment: Build a concept map showing how each of the four macromolecules (carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, nucleic acids) relates to its monomer and main function. Include one food or biological example for each, label at least two chemical features or bonds, and write a 150-word explanation of why carbon-based molecules are central to living systems.

modules.title: Cell Structure & Function
modules.objectives: Compare prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, identify major organelles, and explain how organelle structure supports each organelle's role in cellular function.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Cell Structures
modules.assignment: Draw and label a eukaryotic cell with at least 10 organelles. For each organelle, write one function sentence and one analogy, then explain how three organelles work together to produce or transport a protein.

modules.title: Cell Membrane & Transport
modules.objectives: Explain the fluid mosaic model, distinguish passive transport, active transport, diffusion, facilitated diffusion, and osmosis, and predict movement across membranes in examples.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 5: Membrane Structure & Function
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Fluid Mosaic Model
modules.assignment: Design an experiment to demonstrate osmosis using potato cubes in different salt solutions. Predict and explain expected results. Submit a testable question, hypothesis, variables, procedure, data table template, safety notes, and a conclusion paragraph predicting how mass would change in hypotonic, hypertonic, and isotonic conditions.

modules.title: Cellular Respiration
modules.objectives: Trace the steps of glycolysis, the Krebs cycle, and the electron transport chain; calculate net ATP yield.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Steps of Cellular Respiration
modules.assignment: Create a flow chart of cellular respiration showing glycolysis, Krebs cycle, and electron transport chain. Include location, inputs, outputs, ATP produced, and one sentence explaining why oxygen is required for high ATP yield.

modules.title: Photosynthesis
modules.objectives: Explain the light-dependent and light-independent reactions; describe the role of chlorophyll.
modules.assignment: Write the overall equation for photosynthesis. Explain what would happen to a plant placed in complete darkness for two weeks. Submit a labeled diagram connecting light reactions, the Calvin cycle, reactants, products, and chloroplast structures, then write a 200-word explanation using evidence from the equation and energy flow.

modules.title: Cell Division: Mitosis & Meiosis
modules.titleZh: 细胞分裂：有丝分裂与减数分裂
modules.objectives: Describe the stages of mitosis and meiosis, compare their purposes and outcomes, and explain how chromosome number changes during cell division.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Mitosis Stages
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #12 — Mitosis vs Meiosis
modules.assignment: Create a comparison table for mitosis and meiosis covering purpose, number of divisions, daughter cells, genetic variation, and ploidy. Add a labeled chromosome sketch for one stage of each process and explain why the difference matters for growth or reproduction.

modules.title: Mendelian Genetics
modules.objectives: Apply Mendel's laws of segregation and independent assortment to predict offspring ratios, construct Punnett squares, and interpret genotype and phenotype results.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Mendelian Genetics
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Monohybrid Punnett Square practice
modules.assignment: Set up and solve 3 monohybrid crosses and 1 dihybrid cross. For each, calculate phenotype and genotype ratios, identify the parent genotypes, and write a short interpretation of what the ratios mean biologically.

modules.title: DNA Structure & Replication
modules.objectives: Describe DNA double-helix structure, explain complementary base pairing, and model semi-conservative replication step by step using template and daughter strands.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 14: DNA Structure & Replication
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — DNA Replication
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #10 — DNA Structure & Replication
modules.assignment: Given a template DNA strand, write the complementary strand and show the two daughter DNA molecules after replication. Label the parent strand, new strand, and direction of synthesis, then explain why replication is called semi-conservative.

modules.title: Gene Expression: Transcription & Translation
modules.objectives: Trace the central dogma DNA → RNA → Protein; explain codon, anticodon, and the role of ribosomes.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Transcription & Translation
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #11 — DNA, Hot Pockets & Genetics
modules.assignment: Given a DNA sequence, transcribe it into mRNA and translate it into an amino acid chain using a codon chart. Label codons, anticodons, start and stop signals, then explain how one point mutation could change the resulting protein.

modules.title: Evolution & Natural Selection
modules.objectives: Explain Darwin's theory of natural selection, distinguish directional, stabilizing, and disruptive selection, and apply the four conditions of natural selection to real examples.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 18: Evolution & Natural Selection
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Natural Selection
modules.assignment: Describe a real-world example of natural selection (e.g., antibiotic resistance). Explain how each of the four conditions of natural selection applies. Submit a one-page case study that identifies variation, heritability, differential survival/reproduction, and population change over time, plus a simple diagram showing how allele or trait frequency shifts across generations.

modules.title: Diversity of Life & Classification
modules.objectives: Use binomial nomenclature, classify organisms into domains and kingdoms, and explain how classification reflects shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 20: Phylogenies & the History of Life
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Phylogenetic Trees
modules.assignment: Classify 5 organisms from a provided list using domain, kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species. For each organism, add one sentence explaining which shared trait or evidence supports its placement.

modules.title: Plant Biology
modules.objectives: Identify plant organ systems and tissues; explain vascular transport and the life cycle of flowering plants.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 30: Plant Form & Physiology
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Plant Structures
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Plant Structures & Functions practice
modules.assignment: Label a diagram of a flower showing all major reproductive structures. Explain the role of each structure, trace the path from pollination to seed formation, and compare vascular transport in xylem versus phloem.

modules.title: Human Body Systems
modules.objectives: Describe the major human organ systems, identify their core organs, and explain how multiple systems interact to maintain homeostasis under changing conditions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 33: Animal Form & Function
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Animal Tissues
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Biology #31 — Your Immune System
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Animal Form & Function practice
modules.assignment: Choose any three organ systems. For each, name the major organs, describe its primary function, and explain one specific interaction with another system during exercise, illness, or temperature regulation.

modules.title: Ecology & Ecosystems
modules.objectives: Define biotic/abiotic factors, food webs, and energy flow; explain population dynamics and human impact.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Biology 2e — Ch 44: Ecology & the Biosphere
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Biology — Food Chains & Webs
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Food Chains & Food Webs practice
modules.assignment: Draw a food web for a local ecosystem. Identify the producers, primary consumers, secondary consumers, decomposers, and top predators. Predict what happens if one species is removed. Submit the labeled food web, arrows showing energy flow, a 150-word disruption analysis, and one claim-evidence-reasoning paragraph about ecosystem stability.

questions.question: Which macromolecule serves as the primary energy source for cells?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Carbohydrates. It connects the correct answer to Biology midterm concepts and separates it from plausible distractors.

questions.question: The powerhouse of the cell is the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mitochondrion. This reinforces the Biology midterm concepts idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

questions.question: Which type of transport requires no energy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Passive transport (diffusion). It connects the correct answer to Biology midterm concepts and separates it from plausible distractors.

questions.question: The net ATP yield from one glucose molecule during aerobic respiration is approximately:
questions.explanation: Most biology courses use approximately 30–32 ATP as the net yield from aerobic respiration in eukaryotic cells.

questions.question: In photosynthesis, what molecule absorbs light energy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Chlorophyll. Students need to recognize why Chlorophyll matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

questions.question: In Biology midterm concepts, Mitosis results in:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Two genetically identical daughter cells. It connects the correct answer to Biology midterm concepts and separates it from plausible distractors.

questions.question: In a monohybrid cross of two heterozygous parents (Aa × Aa), the expected phenotype ratio is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 3:1.

questions.question: A prokaryotic cell differs from a eukaryotic cell because it:
options: Stores DNA only in a nucleus
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes do not have a membrane-bound nucleus or other membrane-bound organelles.

questions.question: In Biology midterm concepts, Osmosis is defined as:
options: Movement of solutes across a membrane
Movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from low to high solute
questions.answer: Movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from low to high solute
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from low to high solute.

questions.question: The Krebs cycle takes place in the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mitochondrial matrix. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Biology midterm concepts.

questions.question: In Biology midterm concepts, Meiosis is necessary for:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sexual reproduction (forming gametes). It connects the correct answer to Biology midterm concepts and separates it from plausible distractors.

questions.question: Dominant alleles are conventionally represented by:
questions.explanation: In standard genetics notation, dominant alleles are written with uppercase letters and recessive alleles with lowercase letters.

questions.question: The light reactions of photosynthesis occur in the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Thylakoid membrane. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Biology midterm concepts.

questions.question: In Biology midterm concepts, The Calvin cycle produces:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: G3P (used to build glucose). It connects the correct answer to Biology midterm concepts and separates it from plausible distractors.

questions.question: Enzymes function as biological catalysts by:
options: Decreasing the activation energy of reactions
questions.answer: Decreasing the activation energy of reactions
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Decreasing the activation energy of reactions. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Biology midterm concepts.

questions.question: Which bonds hold the two strands of DNA together?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonds form between complementary base pairs and help hold the DNA strands together.

questions.question: DNA replication is described as semi-conservative because:
options: Only half the DNA is copied
Each new double helix contains one old and one new strand
Half the cell's DNA is conserved
questions.answer: Each new double helix contains one old and one new strand
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Each new double helix contains one old and one new strand.

questions.question: During transcription, DNA is used to produce:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: mRNA. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Biology final concepts.

questions.question: In Biology final concepts, A codon consists of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Three nucleotides on mRNA. This reinforces the Biology final concepts idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

questions.question: Which statement is NOT a requirement of natural selection?
options: Individuals intentionally adapt because they need to
Some variations affect survival or reproduction
questions.answer: Individuals intentionally adapt because they need to
questions.explanation: Natural selection acts on heritable variation; individuals do not intentionally change inherited traits because they need to.

questions.question: Binomial nomenclature uses which two classification levels?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Genus and Species.

questions.question: Xylem and phloem are plant tissues responsible for:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Transporting water/minerals and sugars respectively. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Biology final concepts.

questions.question: In Biology final concepts, Homeostasis refers to:
options: The body's ability to maintain stable internal conditions
questions.answer: The body's ability to maintain stable internal conditions
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The body's ability to maintain stable internal conditions.

questions.question: In an ecological food web, producers are typically:
questions.explanation: Producers make their own food, usually by photosynthesis, and form the base of many food webs.

questions.question: A mutation in a somatic cell:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cannot be passed to offspring. It connects the correct answer to Biology final concepts and separates it from plausible distractors.

questions.question: The three domains of life are:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Bacteria, Archaea, Eukarya. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

questions.question: Antibiotic resistance in bacteria is an example of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Natural selection in action. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Biology final concepts.

questions.question: The primary function of the cell membrane is to:
options: Regulate what enters and exits the cell
Produce all of the cell's ATP
questions.answer: Regulate what enters and exits the cell
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is selectively permeable, helping control movement of materials into and out of the cell.

questions.question: Which process breaks down glucose in the absence of oxygen?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Fermentation (anaerobic respiration). This reinforces the Biology final concepts idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

questions.question: The human nervous system consists of:
options: The brain and spinal cord only
The central and peripheral nervous systems
questions.answer: The central and peripheral nervous systems
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The central and peripheral nervous systems. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

questions.question: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium requires:
options: No mutation, migration, selection, or genetic drift (large random mating population)
questions.answer: No mutation, migration, selection, or genetic drift (large random mating population)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: No mutation, migration, selection, or genetic drift (large random mating population).

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes translate mRNA into polypeptide chains during protein synthesis. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Biology final concepts.

questions.question: Crossing over during meiosis increases:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Genetic diversity in offspring. Students need to recognize why Genetic diversity in offspring matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

questions.question: An organism with two identical alleles for a trait is called:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Homozygous. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

questions.question: The 10% rule in ecology states that:
options: Only 10% of energy transfers to the next trophic level
questions.answer: Only 10% of energy transfers to the next trophic level
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Only 10% of energy transfers to the next trophic level.

quizQuestions.question: Which element is the backbone of all organic molecules?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Carbon. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: In The Chemistry of Life, Proteins are polymers of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Amino acids. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from The Chemistry of Life.

quizQuestions.question: Lipids are primarily used for:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Energy storage and membrane structure. This reinforces the The Chemistry of Life idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: Which element forms the backbone of organic molecules?
quizQuestions.explanation: Carbon can form four covalent bonds, allowing it to build the diverse structures of organic molecules.

quizQuestions.question: Which organelle contains the cell's genetic material?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Nucleus. This reinforces the Cell Structure & Function idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: In Cell Structure & Function, Ribosomes are sites of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Protein synthesis. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Cell Structure & Function.

quizQuestions.question: In Cell Structure & Function, The Golgi apparatus:
options: Modifies and packages proteins for export
quizQuestions.answer: Modifies and packages proteins for export
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Modifies and packages proteins for export. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: Which structure is found in plant cells but NOT animal cells?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cell wall and chloroplast. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Cell Structure & Function.

quizQuestions.question: Where is most eukaryotic genetic material stored?
quizQuestions.explanation: In eukaryotic cells, most DNA is housed inside the nucleus. It connects the correct answer to Cell Structure & Function and separates it from plausible distractors.

quizQuestions.question: The cell membrane is composed of a:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Phospholipid bilayer. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Cell Membrane & Transport.

quizQuestions.question: In Cell Membrane & Transport, Diffusion moves molecules:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: From high to low concentration. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Cell Membrane & Transport.

quizQuestions.question: A cell placed in a hypotonic solution will:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Swell and potentially burst (lyse). It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: The cell membrane's phospholipid arrangement is best described as a:
quizQuestions.explanation: Cell membranes are built mainly from two layers of phospholipids with embedded proteins.

quizQuestions.question: Diffusion moves molecules in which direction?
quizQuestions.explanation: Diffusion is passive movement down a concentration gradient. This reinforces the Cell Membrane & Transport idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: In Cellular Respiration, Glycolysis occurs in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cytoplasm. Students need to recognize why Cytoplasm matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

quizQuestions.question: The overall equation for cellular respiration shows glucose being converted to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: CO₂, water, and ATP. This reinforces the Cellular Respiration idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: The electron transport chain is located on the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cristae (inner mitochondrial membrane). It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: In Cellular Respiration, Fermentation is used when:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Oxygen is absent or limited. This reinforces the Cellular Respiration idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: Where does glycolysis occur in the cell?
quizQuestions.explanation: Glycolysis takes place in the cytoplasm before later stages of respiration occur in the mitochondria.

quizQuestions.question: The overall equation for photosynthesis shows:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: CO₂ + H₂O + light → glucose + O₂. Students need to recognize why CO₂ + H₂O + light → glucose + O₂ matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

quizQuestions.question: Chlorophyll absorbs light primarily in which colors?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Red and blue. This reinforces the Photosynthesis idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: The light-independent reactions (Calvin cycle) take place in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Stroma of the chloroplast. It connects the correct answer to Photosynthesis and separates it from plausible distractors.

quizQuestions.question: In photosynthesis, carbon dioxide and water plus light energy are converted into:
quizQuestions.explanation: The overall photosynthesis equation can be summarized as CO₂ + H₂O + light → glucose + O₂.

quizQuestions.question: Short response: Which gas do plants absorb for photosynthesis?
quizQuestions.explanation: A correct response identifies "Carbon dioxide" and connects it to the module context.

quizQuestions.question: The correct order of mitosis phases is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Prophase, Metaphase, Anaphase, Telophase. Students need to recognize why Prophase, Metaphase, Anaphase, Telophase matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

quizQuestions.question: During anaphase, sister chromatids:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Are pulled to opposite poles. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Cell Division: Mitosis & Meiosis.

quizQuestions.question: Meiosis II is most similar to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mitosis. This reinforces the Cell Division: Mitosis & Meiosis idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: How many haploid cells result from one round of meiosis?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 4. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Cell Division: Mitosis & Meiosis.

quizQuestions.question: In Cell Division: Mitosis & Meiosis, Cytokinesis refers to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The division of the cytoplasm. Students need to recognize why The division of the cytoplasm matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

quizQuestions.question: Gregor Mendel conducted his genetics experiments on:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pea plants. It connects the correct answer to Mendelian Genetics and separates it from plausible distractors.

quizQuestions.question: A heterozygous genotype contains:
options: One dominant and one recessive allele
quizQuestions.answer: One dominant and one recessive allele
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: One dominant and one recessive allele. This reinforces the Mendelian Genetics idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: The law of segregation states that:
options: Alleles for different traits assort independently
Each gamete receives one allele for each trait
quizQuestions.answer: Each gamete receives one allele for each trait
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Each gamete receives one allele for each trait. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Mendelian Genetics.

quizQuestions.question: Gregor Mendel conducted his classic genetics experiments using:
quizQuestions.explanation: Mendel studied inheritance patterns in pea plants, including traits such as flower color and seed shape.

quizQuestions.question: The base pairing rule in DNA is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: A-T and G-C. This reinforces the DNA Structure & Replication idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: DNA helicase unwinds the double helix by:
options: Breaking hydrogen bonds between base pairs
quizQuestions.answer: Breaking hydrogen bonds between base pairs
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Breaking hydrogen bonds between base pairs. It connects the correct answer to DNA Structure & Replication and separates it from plausible distractors.

quizQuestions.question: DNA polymerase adds nucleotides in which direction?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 5' to 3'. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in DNA Structure & Replication.

quizQuestions.question: In DNA Structure & Replication, The sugar in DNA is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Deoxyribose. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from DNA Structure & Replication.

quizQuestions.question: Which DNA base-pairing rule is correct?
quizQuestions.explanation: In DNA, adenine pairs with thymine, and guanine pairs with cytosine. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: The central dogma of molecular biology is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: DNA → RNA → Protein. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: Transcription occurs in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Nucleus. Students need to recognize why Nucleus matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

quizQuestions.question: In mRNA, thymine (T) is replaced by:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Uracil. It connects the correct answer to Gene Expression: Transcription & Translation and separates it from plausible distractors.

quizQuestions.question: Which sequence best represents the central dogma of molecular biology?
quizQuestions.explanation: The central dogma describes information flow from DNA to RNA to protein. It connects the correct answer to Gene Expression: Transcription & Translation and separates it from plausible distractors.

quizQuestions.question: Where does transcription of nuclear DNA occur in a eukaryotic cell?
quizQuestions.explanation: In eukaryotic cells, transcription occurs in the nucleus before mRNA travels to ribosomes for translation.

quizQuestions.question: Darwin's book proposing evolution by natural selection was called:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: On the Origin of Species. It connects the correct answer to Evolution & Natural Selection and separates it from plausible distractors.

quizQuestions.question: Fitness in evolutionary terms means:
options: Ability to survive and reproduce successfully
quizQuestions.answer: Ability to survive and reproduce successfully
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Ability to survive and reproduce successfully. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Evolution & Natural Selection.

quizQuestions.question: In Evolution & Natural Selection, Vestigial structures are:
options: Remnants of structures that served a function in ancestors
quizQuestions.answer: Remnants of structures that served a function in ancestors
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Remnants of structures that served a function in ancestors.

quizQuestions.question: Genetic drift has the greatest effect in:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Small populations. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Evolution & Natural Selection.

quizQuestions.question: Which book introduced Darwin's theory of evolution by natural selection?
quizQuestions.explanation: Darwin published his theory of evolution by natural selection in On the Origin of Species.

quizQuestions.question: The correct order of taxonomic hierarchy from broadest to most specific is:
options: Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom, Domain
Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Kingdom, Domain, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Domain, Phylum, Kingdom, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
quizQuestions.answer: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species.

quizQuestions.question: In Homo sapiens, "sapiens" is the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Species. The choice fits the prompt because it applies the central rule or concept from Diversity of Life & Classification.

quizQuestions.question: Archaea are similar to bacteria in that they are both:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Prokaryotes. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Diversity of Life & Classification.

quizQuestions.question: From broadest to most specific, what is the correct taxonomic hierarchy?
options: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom, Domain
Kingdom, Domain, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Domain, Phylum, Kingdom, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
quizQuestions.answer: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
quizQuestions.explanation: Taxonomic levels move from the broad domain level down to the specific species level.

quizQuestions.question: In Plant Biology, Xylem transports:
options: Water and minerals from roots upward
quizQuestions.answer: Water and minerals from roots upward
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Water and minerals from roots upward. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: In Plant Biology, Stomata are openings that:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Regulate gas exchange in leaves. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Plant Biology.

quizQuestions.question: The reproductive structures of a flowering plant are found in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Flower. The explanation links the answer to the underlying process or evidence expected in Plant Biology.

quizQuestions.question: Pollination is the transfer of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pollen from stamen to pistil. Students need to recognize why Pollen from stamen to pistil matches the evidence in the question and why nearby options do not.

quizQuestions.question: In Plant Biology, Phloem transports:
options: Sugars (products of photosynthesis) throughout the plant
quizQuestions.answer: Sugars (products of photosynthesis) throughout the plant
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sugars (products of photosynthesis) throughout the plant.

quizQuestions.question: The primary function of the respiratory system is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Gas exchange (O₂ in, CO₂ out). It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: The site of gas exchange in the lungs is the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Alveoli. It names the scientific relationship being tested and distinguishes it from related vocabulary.

quizQuestions.question: Insulin is produced by the pancreas to:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Lower blood glucose levels. This reinforces the Human Body Systems idea behind the item instead of rewarding a surface-level guess.

quizQuestions.question: What is the primary function of the respiratory system?
options: Gas exchange: oxygen in and carbon dioxide out
quizQuestions.answer: Gas exchange: oxygen in and carbon dioxide out
quizQuestions.explanation: The respiratory system brings oxygen into the body and removes carbon dioxide.

quizQuestions.question: Producers in an ecosystem are:
options: Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis
quizQuestions.answer: Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Organisms that make their own food via photosynthesis.

quizQuestions.question: In Ecology & Ecosystems, The 10% energy rule means:
options: Only 10% of energy passes to the next trophic level
quizQuestions.answer: Only 10% of energy passes to the next trophic level
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Only 10% of energy passes to the next trophic level.

quizQuestions.question: A niche is best described as:
options: An organism's role and resource use in its ecosystem
The number of organisms in a habitat
quizQuestions.answer: An organism's role and resource use in its ecosystem
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: An organism's role and resource use in its ecosystem.

quizQuestions.question: Biodiversity is important because:
options: Greater diversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience
quizQuestions.answer: Greater diversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Greater diversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience.

quizQuestions.question: Which description best fits producers in an ecosystem?
options: Organisms that make their own food, often through photosynthesis
Organisms that break down all rocks
quizQuestions.answer: Organisms that make their own food, often through photosynthesis
quizQuestions.explanation: Producers are autotrophs that supply energy to other organisms in an ecosystem.
